claim_status: Build-readiness dependency graph only; no task approval, no readiness promotion, no broad M1 implementation claim, no developer preview claim, no beta claim, no stable claim, no production claim, no Chrome-class claim, no performance claim, no compatibility claim, no security claim, no accessibility claim, no release readiness claim, and no daily-driver claim.

nodes.summary: Contained M0 and no-claim evidence work are authorized while broad implementation remains blocked.
nodes.claim_boundary: No broad M1, preview, beta, stable, production, Chrome-class, performance, compatibility, security, accessibility, release, or daily-driver claim.

nodes.summary: Sandbox probe targets, a no-claim probe-package template, and a no-claim sandbox readiness-review template are checked as planning evidence, but packaged expected-deny probes, platform policy capture, and owner-reviewed sandbox readiness are missing.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: Documentation and research may continue on ADR-0009 evidence, source provenance, legal, compatibility, performance, security, and maintenance records.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before importing release-path browser-engine code, approving source adoption, changing source-strategy status, or making a source-approval claim.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: Task-scoped reproduction evidence may run after owner task approval while preserving source-tree cleanliness, retained logs, and no-claim readiness-review boundaries.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before calling same-host runs independent, treating the checked no-claim readiness-review template as owner-reviewed evidence, promoting PB-009, or claiming release confidence, implementation, production, or Chrome-class status.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: Bounded IPC, authority-model, and expected-deny sandbox probe prototypes may proceed as contained M0 evidence.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before production IPC, renderer-security, site-isolation, sandbox-readiness, or security-gate claims.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: No-claim benchmark corpus, runner, trace, artifact, browser-pin, resource-attribution, and benchmark readiness-review contracts may continue.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before benchmark-ready pins, equal-workload public comparisons, raw result claims, owner-reviewed benchmark readiness, speed, memory, energy, or Chrome-class language.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: Toolkit-neutral UI, framework bake-off, page-surface, component fixture, input, IME, and accessibility prototypes may continue as isolated reference evidence.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before selecting a toolkit, accepting ADR-0013, ADR-0014, or ADR-0016, approving UI-GATE-7, or claiming release-path UI readiness.

parallel_no_claim_lanes.allowed_parallel_work: Profile/session schemas, research-package labs, incident tabletop records, and backup ownership candidates may proceed as no-claim evidence.
parallel_no_claim_lanes.must_stop_before: Stop before real profile migration, production signing, real updater, disclosure authority, incident closure, legal approval, owner coverage, production authority, or any production-authority claim.
